questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A rectangular conducting loop carrying a current i1 is situated near a long straight wire carrying a steady current i2. The wire is parallel to one of the sides of the loop and is placed in the same plane as that of the loop as shown in the figure. Then, the current loop will
options: rotate about an axis parallel to the wire
questions.solution:
F=μ02πi1i2dl
where d is distance between conductors

∴  F∝1d
 

Hence, lesser the distance, more is the force In the given circuit force of attraction is more between EF and AD that of repulsion between EF and BC, hence current loop will move towards the wire.

F=μ02πi1i2dl

∴ F∝1d
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For c=2a and a<b<c, the magnetic field at the point P will be zero when
questions.solution:
Magnetic field at the centre

      b = μ 0 I 2 c ⊗ + μ 0 I 2 b 3 4 ⊙ + μ 0 I 2 a 3 4 ⊗

As per problem :

      0 = μ 0 I 2 1 c - 3 4 b + 3 4 a ⇒ 3 4 1 b - 1 a = 1 c

If c=2a,

      3 4 1 b - 1 a = 1 2 a ⇒ a = 5 3 b
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle of specific charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mtext mathvariant="italic">q</mtext><mtext mathvariant="italic">m</mtext></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi>π</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>kg</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is projected from the origin towards positive x-axis with a velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> in a uniform magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mover><mtext>k</mtext><mo>^</mo></mover><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>. The velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>v</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math> of the particle after time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>12</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> will be (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Time period,  T = 2 π m qB = 2 π π × 2 = 1 s

Thus, the particle will be at point P after t=112 s



v → = 1 0 cos 3 0 i ^ + sin  3 0 j ^

      v → = 1 0 3 2 i ^ + 1 2 j ^ = 5 3 i ^ + j ^ m s - 1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A non-conducting thin disc of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> rotates about its axis with an angular velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>w</mi></math>. The surface charge density on the disc varies with the distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> from the center as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>σ</mi><mfenced><mi>r</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>σ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mi>r</mi><mi>R</mi></mfrac></mfenced><mi>β</mi></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>σ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are constants. If the magnetic induction at the center is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mfrac><mn>9</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac></mfenced><msub><mi>μ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><msub><mi>σ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mi>w</mi><mi>R</mi></math>, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Consider a thin ring of thickness dr at r from the centre of the disc.

∴  dB=μ0×dI2r

but dI=dQT=dQ2π×ω=σ×2πrdr2π×ω=σωrdr

B=∫0Rμ02r×σωrdr=∫0Rωμ02r×σ01+rRβrdr

∴  B=ωμ0σ02∫0R01+rRβdr=ωμ0σ022+β1+βR

Given B=910μ0σ0wR

∴  ωμ0σ022+β1+βR=910μ0σ0wR

∴   5β+10=9β+9⇒β=14
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A magnetic needle lying parallel to a magnetic field requires \(W\) units of work to turn it through \(60^{\circ}\). The torque required to maintain the needle in this position will be
questions.solution:
According to the question, work done required to rotate magnetic needle from \(\theta_1=0^{\circ}\) to \(\theta_2=60^{\circ}\).
\(W=M B\left(\cos \theta_1-\cos \theta_2\right)\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =M B\left(\cos 0^{\circ}-\cos 60^{\circ}\right) \\
& =M B\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right) \\
W & =\frac{M B}{2} \Rightarrow M B=2 W \quad \ldots (i) \\
\therefore \text {Torque, } \tau & =M B \sin 60^{\circ}=M B \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\
& =2 W \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}  \quad \text { [from Eq. (i)] } \\
& =W \sqrt{3}=\sqrt{3} W
\end{aligned}\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two moving coil galvanometer, $X$ and $Y$ have coils with resistance $10 \Omega$ and $14 \Omega$ cross-sectional areas $4.8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2$ and $2.4 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2$, number of turns 30 and 45 respectively. They are placed in magnetic field of $0.25 \mathrm{~T}$ and $0.50 \mathrm{~T}$ respectively. Then, the ratio of their current sensitivities and the ratio of their voltage sensitivities are respectively
questions.solution:
There is no information regarding spring constant.
So, let's assume their spring constants are same.
Current sensitivity, $I_S=\frac{N B A}{k}$
So,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{I_{S_1}}{I_{S_2}} & =\frac{N_1 B_1 A_1 k_2}{N_2 B_2 A_2 k_1} \\
& =\frac{30 \times 0.25 \times 4.8 \times 10^{-3}}{45 \times 0.5 \times 24 \times 10^{-3}} \times\left(\frac{1}{1}\right) \\
\frac{I_{S_1}}{I_{S_2}} & =2 / 3
\end{aligned}
$$
Voltage sensitivity, $V_S=N B A / R k$
So,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{V_{S_1}}{V_{S_2}} & =\frac{N_1 B_1 A_1 k_2 R_2}{N_2 B_2 A_2 k_1 R_1} \\
& =\frac{2}{3} \times\left(\frac{R_2}{R_1}\right)=\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{14}{10}=\frac{14}{15}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A charge $q$ enters a region having electric field $\mathbf{E}$ and magnetic field $\mathbf{B}$ with velocity $\mathbf{v}$. If it continues to move with the same velocity, then which of the following statement is not true
questions.solution:
Lorentz force on electron,
$$
F=q(\mathbf{E}+\mathbf{v}+\mathbf{B})
$$
For regular motion, $F=0$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad \mathbf{E}+(\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B})=0
$$
$\Rightarrow \mathbf{E} \|(\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B})$, also $[\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}$ is perpendicular to both $v$ and $\mathbf{B}]$
Now, $\mathbf{E} \perp \mathbf{v}$ and $\mathbf{E} \perp \mathbf{B}$ [(i) and (ii) are right]
$$
\Rightarrow \quad \mathbf{E} \cdot \mathbf{v}=0 \text { and } \mathbf{E} \cdot \mathbf{B}=0
$$
Now, if $\quad \mathbf{v} \cdot \mathbf{B}=0 \Rightarrow \mathbf{v} \perp \mathbf{B}$
So, $\mathbf{E} \perp \mathbf{v} \perp \mathbf{B}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathbf{E}=\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B} \\
& \mathbf{E} \times \mathbf{B}=(\mathbf{V} \times \mathbf{B}) \times \mathbf{B} \\
& v \times \mathbf{B}=v B \hat{\mathbf{n}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad(\mathbf{v} B) \times \mathbf{B}=\mathbf{v}(\mathbf{B} \cdot \mathbf{B}) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \mathbf{v}=\frac{\mathbf{E} \times \mathbf{B}}{\mathbf{B} \cdot \mathbf{B}} \\
& \mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{E}=\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}=0 \neq \mathbf{B} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
[iii) is right]
Now, $\quad \mathbf{E}=\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}$
$$
\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{E}=\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}=0 \neq \mathbf{B}
$$
So, option (d) is wrong.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two magnetic dipoles X and Y are kept at a distance d apart, with their axes perpendicular to each other. The dipole moment of Y is twice that of X. P is a point along the horizontal line which is at the midpoint of d. What is the magnitude of the force of particle of charge q passing through P at angle θ=45°? (d is very much larger in comparison to dipole)
questions.solution:
B1=2μ04π·M(d/2)3; B2=μ04π·2M(d/2)3
⇒B1=B2

∴ Net magnetic field will be at 45°.

∴The direction of Bnet and velocity of charge is same, hence charge will experience no force.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A steady current flow in a long wire. It is bent into a circular loop of one turn and the magnetic field at the centre of the coil is<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math>. If the same wire is bent into a circular loop of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> turns, the magnetic field at the centre of the coil is
questions.solution:
The magnetic field a circular coil (for n number of loops)

B=μ0ni2r

When, n1=1, n2=2

2πr1=n×2πr2

⇒ r1r2=n1

So B1B2=μ0n1i2r1μ0n2i2r2

BB2=n1n2.r1r2

BB2=1n.1n

⇒ B2=n2B
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A wire of certain length carries a steady current. It is first bent to form a circular coil of one turn. The same wire is then bent to form a circular coil of three turns. The ratio of magnetic inductions at the centre of the coil in the two cases is
questions.solution:
Magnetic induction at the centre of the coil with $\mathrm{N}$ turns is given by
$B_{\mathrm{N}}=\left(\frac{\mu_0 I}{2 r}\right) \mathrm{N}$
Where $r$ is the radius of each coil is, $I$ is the current flowing through.
$\therefore$ for single loop coil $2 \pi r=L$
$B_1=\frac{\mu_0 I(1)}{2\left(\frac{L}{2 \pi}\right)}=\frac{\mu_0 I \pi}{L}$
\& For the three-loop coil $(2 \pi r) 3=L$
$B_3=\frac{\mu_0 I(3)}{2\left(\frac{L}{6 \pi}\right)}=\frac{9 \mu_0 I \pi}{L}$
Or $\frac{B_1}{B_2}=\frac{1}{9}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two concentric circular coils $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ have radii $20 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ respectively lie in the same plane. The current in coil A is $0.5 \mathrm{~A}$ in anticlockwise direction. The current in coil B so that net field at the common centre is zero, is
questions.solution:
Magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop
$$
\mathrm{B}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{NI}}{2 \mathrm{R}}
$$
Net Magneitc field $B_{\text {net }}=0$
(given)
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow B_{\text {net }}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{~N}_1 \times 0.5}{2 \times(0.2)}-\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{~N}_1 \times \mathrm{x}}{2 \times(0 \cdot 1)} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{~N}_1 \mathrm{x}}{0.2}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{~N}_1 0.5}{0.4} \\ \therefore \quad & x=\frac{0.5 \times 0.2}{0.4} \\ & =0.25 \mathrm{~A} \text { in clockwise direction }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two identical bar magnets of magnetic moment $M$ each, are placed along $X$ and $Y$-axes, respectively at a distance $d$ from the origin (as shown in the figure). The origin lies on perpendicular bisector of magnet placed on $X$-axis and on the magnetic axis of magnet placed on $Y$-axis. If the magnitude of total magnetic field at the origin is $B=\alpha\left[\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{M}{d^3}\right]$, then the value of constant $\alpha$ will be $\langle d>>l$, where $l$ is the length of the bar magnets and direction of $\mathrm{N}$ to $\mathrm{S}$ in magnets is opposite with respect to each other)
questions.solution: Magnetic field due to axial magnet, $$ \mathbf{B}_1=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{2 M}{d^3} \quad \text { (+Y direction) } $$ Magnetic field due to equatorial magnet, $$ \mathbf{B}_2=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{M}{d^3} \quad \text { (+Y direction) } $$ So, effective magnetic field $$ \begin{aligned} & \mathbf{B}=\mathbf{B}_1+\mathbf{B}_2=3 \frac{\mu_0 M}{4 \pi d^3} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \alpha=3 \\ & \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A long straight wire carrying a current of $25 \mathrm{~A}$ rests on the table. Another wire PQ of length $1 \mathrm{~m}$ and mass $2.5 \mathrm{~g}$ carries the same current but in the opposite direction. The wire PQ is free to slide up and down. To what height will wire $\mathrm{PQ}$ rise? $\left(\mu_0=4 \pi \times 10^{-7}\right.$ SI unit $)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A rectangular loop of metallic wire is of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and breadth <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> and carries a current <math><mi>i</mi></math>. The magnetic field at the centre of the loop is
questions.solution:
From figure it is clear that



BAB=BCD

=μ0i4πb2sin⁡ϕ1+sin⁡ϕ2=μ04π⋅4ib⋅aa2+b2

BBC=BDA=μ04π⋅4ia⋅ba2+b2

∴ B=BAB+BBC+BCD+BDA

B=2μ04π⋅4ib⋅aa2+b2+2μ04π⋅4ia⋅ba2+b2

B=μ04π⋅2×4ia2+b2ab+ba

B=μ04π⋅8ia2+b2ab
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin flexible wire of length $L$ is connected to two adjacent fixed points and carries a current $I$ in the clockwise direction as shown. When the system is put in uniform magnetic field of strength $B$ going into the plane of the paper, the wire take the shape of a circle. The tension in the wire, after acquiring the circular shape is
questions.solution:
Given that, the length of wire $=L$ Current passing through ring $=I$ Let $R$ be the radius of ring. Then, $2 \pi R=L$ $$ R=\frac{L}{2 \pi}...(i) $$ Consider an element of ring $A B$ which subtends an angle $\Delta \theta$ at the centre $O$, such that, $A B=\Delta l=R \Delta \theta$...(ii) If $T$ be the tension at point $P$ as shown in figure.
We know that, force acting on element $A B$ in uniform magnetic field, $$ F=I B \Delta l $$ ... (iii) (radially outward) By resolving tension into sine and cosine components $T \cos \frac{\Delta \theta}{2}$ is acting equal and opposite. So, it is cancelled.
But sum of sine component is equal and opposite to $F$. $\therefore$ At equilibrium, we get $$ 2 T \sin \frac{\Delta \theta}{2}=F $$ As $\frac{\Delta \theta}{2}$ is very small angle, so, $\sin \frac{\Delta \theta}{2}=\frac{\Delta \theta}{2}$ By substituting the values, we get $$ \begin{aligned} 2 T \frac{\Delta \theta}{2} & =I B \Delta l \Rightarrow T \Delta \theta=I B R \Delta \theta \\ T & =I B R...(i) \end{aligned} $$ Using Eqs. (i) and (iv), we get $$ T=I B L / 2 \pi $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A wire loop carrying current i is placed in the x-y plane as shown in the figure. If an external uniform magnetic field B→=Bi^ is switched on, then the torque acting on the loop is
questions.solution:
∵   Magnetic dipole moment of current loop is M → =I A →

here area of loop A = Area of sector of circle - area of ∆

13πa2-12a cos60°×a sin60°×2



A = π a 2 × 1 3 - 2 × 1 2 × a 2 3 2 a . = π a 2 3 - 3 4 a 2 , A → = π 3 - 3 4 a 2 k ^

∵   torque on loop placed in uniform magnetic field is

τ → = M → × B → = I A → × B → = I π 3 - 3 4 a 2 k ^ × B i ^ = IB a 2 π 3 - 3 4 j ^
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two infinitely long straight wires $A$ and $B$, each carrying current $I$ are placed on $x$ and $y$-axis, respectively. The current in wires $A$ and $B$ flow along $-\hat{\mathbf{i}}$ and $\hat{\mathbf{j}}$ directions respectively. The force on a charged particle having charge $q$, moving from position, $\mathbf{r}=d(\hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}})$ with velocity $\mathbf{v}=\hat{v}$ is
questions.solution:
The given situation for two current carrying wires are shown in the figures.
Position vector of charge particle, $$ \mathbf{r}=d(\hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}})=d \hat{\mathbf{i}}+d \hat{\mathbf{j}} $$ The magnetic field intensity due to the wire along $-\hat{\mathbf{i}}$ direction, $$ \mathbf{B}_2=\frac{\mu_0}{2 \pi} \cdot \frac{Y}{d}(-\hat{\mathbf{k}}) $$ Similarly, magnetic field intensity due to wire along $\hat{\mathbf{j}}$ direction. $$ \mathbf{B}_2=\frac{\mu_0}{2 \pi} \cdot \frac{r}{d}(-\hat{\mathbf{k}}) $$ $\therefore$ Resultant magnetic field at point $P$ due to both the wires, $$ \begin{aligned} & \mathbf{B}=\mathbf{B}_2+\mathbf{B}_2=2 \cdot \frac{\mu_0}{2 \pi} \cdot \frac{Y}{d}(-\hat{\mathbf{k}}) \\ & \mathbf{B}=\frac{\mu_0}{\pi} \cdot \frac{I}{d}(-\hat{\mathbf{k}}) \end{aligned} $$ $\therefore$ Force on the charged particles, $$ \mathbf{F}=q(\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B})=q\left[v \hat{\mathbf{i}} \times \frac{\mu_0}{\pi} \frac{Y}{d} \cdot(-\hat{\mathbf{k}})\right]=\frac{\mu_0 Y q V}{\pi \cdot d} \cdot \hat{\mathbf{j}} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find the magnitude of the magnetic field at the center of an equilateral triangular loop of side length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> which is carrying a current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>. (Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mi>NA</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Magnetic field due to wire BC is ⇒B=μ04πiℓ×2332+32⊗

Net magnetic field at centre  = 3B    

=μ04πiℓ×2332+323⊗

=μ04π1ℓ6(1)3=10-7×101×18=18×10-6T
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A compass needle free to turn in a horizontal plane is placed at the centre of a circular coil of <math> <mrow> <mtext>30</mtext></mrow> </math> turns and radius <math> <mrow> <mtext>12 cm</mtext></mrow> </math>. The coil is in a vertical plane making an angle of <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mtext>45</mtext></mrow> <mo>∘</mo> </msup> </mrow> </math> with the magnetic meridian. The needle points west to east when the current in the coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>0</mtext><mtext>.35</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>A</mtext></math>. The horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>) at that location is
questions.solution:
Given, number of turns in the coil n = 30

Current in the coil I = 0.35 A

Radius of circular coil = 12 cm = 0.12 m

      

Let N - S be the line of magnetic of 45o with the magnetic meridian. C1 and C2 be the plane of coil. The needle points west to east. The magnetic field produced due to the coil is B. The direction of B is along the axis of coil i.e., it makes an angle of 45o with east. The needle points west to east only if the direction of magnetic field B is at 45o from east.

Use the formula, magnetic field produced by a current carrying coil

                    B = μ 0 4 π · 2 π I r = 1 0 × 2 × 3 . 1 4 × 0 . 3 5 × 3 0 0 . 1 2

                        = 1.83 x 10-6 x 30 = 5.49 x 10-5 T

The horizontal component of magnetic field

                    H = B sin 45o = 5.49 x 10-5 × 1 2

                                          = 3.9 x 10-5 T.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two circular concentric loops of radii r1=20 cm, r2=30 cm are placed in the X-Y plane as shown in the figure. A current I=7 A is flowing through them. The magnetic moment of this loop system is
questions.solution:
Here, magnetic moment due to loop 1

M1=iA1

 =iπr12

 =7π0.202=0.28π

Similarly magnetic moment due to loop-2

M2=iA2=iπr22

 =7π 0.302=0.63π

Net magnetic moment

 =M1-M2

 =0.63π-0.28π=1.1 A m2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A $10 \Omega$ coil of 180 turns and diameter $4 \mathrm{~cm}$ is placed in a uniform magnetic field so that the magnetic flux is maximum through the coil's cross-sectional area. When the field is suddenly removed a charge of $360 \mu \mathrm{C}$ flows through a $618 \Omega$ galvanometer connected to the coil, find the magnetic field.
questions.solution:
Given, resistance of a coil, $R=10 \Omega$ number of turns in the coil, $N=180$ and diameter of the coil $=4 \mathrm{~cm}=4 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}$
Magnetic flux associated with the coil is maximum.
Hence, $\theta=0^{\circ}$
$\therefore$ Magnetic flux, $\phi=B A$
When magnetic field is suddenly removed, then some charge flows through the galvanometer.
Given, resistance of a galvanometer, $R_g=618 \Omega$ and charge flowing through the galvanometer, $q=360 \mu \mathrm{C}=360 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{C}$
As $\quad q=I \cdot \Delta t$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad q=\frac{e}{R_{e q}} \Delta t=\frac{\Delta \phi}{\Delta t} \cdot \frac{\Delta t}{R_{e q}} \quad\left(\because e=N \frac{\Delta \phi}{\Delta t}\right)
$$
So, for $N$ turns, $q=\frac{N}{R_{\text {eq }}} \Delta \phi$
Here, $R_{e q}=R+R_g$
Putting the given values, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 360 \times 10^{-6}=\frac{180}{(618+10)} \cdot(B A-0) \\
& 2 \times 10^{-6} \times 628=B \cdot \pi \times\left(2 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2 \quad\left[\because A=\pi r^2\right] \\
\Rightarrow & B=\frac{2 \times 10^{-6} \times 628}{3.14 \times 4 \times 10^{-4}}=1 \mathrm{~T}
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, the magnetic field is $1 \mathrm{~T}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A long straight wire carrying current $16 \mathrm{~A}$ is bent at $90^{\circ}$ such that half of the wire lies along the positive $x$-axis and other half lies along the positive $y$-axis. What is the magnitude of the magnetic field at the point, $\mathbf{r}=(-2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+0 \hat{\mathbf{j}}) \mathrm{mm}$ ? (Assume, $\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi}=10^{-7} \mathrm{H} \mathrm{m}^{-1}$ )
questions.solution:
According to the question, a long straight wire is bent at $90^{\circ}$ as shown in the figure,
Where, current flowing through the wire, $I=16 \mathrm{~A}$ $$ \mathbf{r}=\mathrm{OP}=(-2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+0 \hat{\mathbf{j}}) \mathrm{mm} $$ Magnitude of the magnetic field at point $P$ due to current carrying wire along $x$-axis is zero because pont $P$ lies in the direction of conducting wire along $x$-axis. $\therefore$ Magnitude of the magnetic field due to current carrying wire along $y$-axis at point $P$ is given by $$ B=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \cdot \frac{I}{r} $$ Putting the given values in above relation, we get $$ \begin{aligned} & =\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \times \frac{16}{2 \times 10^{-3}} \quad\left[\because r=2 \mathrm{~mm}=2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}\right] \\ & =10^{-7} \times 8 \times 10^3=0.8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~T}=0.8 \mathrm{mT} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The magnetic field at the centre \(\mathrm{C}\) of the arrangement shown in figure is
questions.solution:
According to diagram in the question, magnetic field at the centre \(C\) will be sum of the magnetic field due to straight part \(B_1\) and magnetic field due to circular part \(B_2\).
\(\because\) From Maxwell's right hand thumb rule direction of both magnetic field at the point \(C\) will be same and above the plane of paper.
So, \(B_C=B_1+B_2=\frac{\mu_0 i}{4 \pi r}+\frac{\mu_0 i}{4 r}=\frac{\mu_0 i}{4 \pi r}(1+\pi)\)
Hence, the magnetic field at the centre \(C\) of the arrangement shown in figure is, \(\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi r} i(1+\pi)\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Three long wires, with identical currents, either directly into or directly out of the page, are placed at three corners of a square in four different arrangements as shown. The correct order of the magnitude of the net magnetic field at the centre of the square is

I 

II 

III 

IV
questions.solution:
In figure II and III field due to three wires add up. Hence, the magnitude is same. In figure I and IV field due to two wires cancel and the field is only due to third wire.

∴BI= BI V < BI I = BI I I
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A dielectric circular disc of radíus $R$ carries a unform surface charge density $\sigma$. If it rotates about its axis with angular velocity $\omega$, the magnetic fíeld at the cente of dísc is :
questions.solution:
Let us assumed a ring like element of the disc of radius $r$ and thickness $d r$. If $\sigma$ is the surface charge density, then the charge on the element. $$ d q=\sigma(2 \pi r) d r $$ Current $d i$ associated with rotating charge $d q$ is $$ d i=\frac{d q}{T}=\frac{d q \omega}{2 \pi} \quad\left(\because T=\frac{2 \pi}{\omega}\right) \ldots $$
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $$ \Rightarrow \quad d i=\sigma \omega r d r $$ As, the magnetic field $d B$ at centre due to the element, $$ \begin{aligned} & d B=\frac{\mu_0 d i}{2 r}=\frac{\mu_0 \sigma \omega r d r}{2 r} \Rightarrow d B=\frac{\mu_0 \sigma \omega}{2} d r \\ & \Rightarrow B_{\text {net }}=\frac{\mu_0 \sigma \omega}{2} \int_0^R d r \quad \Rightarrow B_{\text {net }}=\frac{\mu_0 \sigma \omega R}{2} \\ & \end{aligned} $$ Hence, the correct option is (2).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A charged particle moves with some initial velocity along the direction of external magnetic field $B$. Now, if we apply uniform field perpendicular to the magnetic field, then the trajectory of the charged particle will be
questions.solution:
As charged particle enters region of electric field, its' net velocity is inclined at some angle to $B$.
So, path of charged particle is a helix.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The pole strength of a horse shoe magnet is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>90</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> and distance between the poles is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. The magnetic induction at mid point of the line joining the poles is,
questions.solution:
Magnetic induction B due to a monopole is given by

B = μ04πmr2 

{ μ0 = absolute permeability of the medium, m = pole strength, r = distance of the point from pole where B is to be calculated}

Magnetic induction due to both the poles at the middle point O will be equal in magnitude and have same direction.

∴ BO = 2B

Where B is the magnetic induction at O due to a single pole.

BO=2× μ04π903×10-22⇒ BO = 2×10-7×909×10-4⇒BO= 2×10-2 T
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics

lR=θ

B=μ0iθ2π2R

B=μ0ilθ24πl2

B∝θ2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following statements

I. If a piece of bar magnet is broken into two equally long pieces, then the pieces will not lose the magnetic properties.
II. Magnetic properties of a substance lie at the atomic level.
Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
A magnetic monopole does not exist, i.e., even if we break a magnet it will behave as two magnets, and we cannot isolate a magnetic monopole.

If we keep on dividing the magnet till the atomic level the magnetic property will still be there, thus the magnetic property of a substance lie in the atomic level.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A circular loop of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> carrying current <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> is kept in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>XZ</mi></math>  plane. A uniform and constant magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced></math> exists in the region (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math>- a positive constant) . Then the magnitude of the torque acting on the loop will be
questions.solution:
Magnetic dipole moment, M→=±IπR2j^

τ→=M→×B→=±IπR2j^×B0i^+2B0j^+3B0k^

τ→=±IπR2B0-k^+3i^

questions.q: A circular wire has current density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>J</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>10</mn></msup><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> is the radial distance, out of the wire radius is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The end-to-end potential applied to the wire is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. How much energy (in joule) is converted to thermal energy in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>?
questions.solution:
Given: Current density J=2×1010r2 A m-2 , potential V=50 V, time t=100 s

Energy is given as E=VIt

Current I=∫02×10-3JdA=∫02×10-32×1010r2×2πr dr

(Since A=2πr)

 I=2×10102π∫02×10-3 r3 dr=4π×1010r442×10-3

E=VIt=50×16π×100=800π .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle of mass $1 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~kg}$ and charge $1 \times 10^{-16} \mathrm{C}$ enters the uniform magnetic field within the solenoid, at speed $1000 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. The velocity vector makcs an angle $60^{\circ}$ with the axis of solenoid. The solenoid has 5000 turns along its length and carries current $5 \mathrm{~A}$. The number of revolution the particle makes along the helical path within the solenoid by the time it emerges from solenoid's opposite end is
questions.solution:
The given situation is shown below.
Given, $\begin{aligned} v & =1000 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\ q & =10^{-16} \mathrm{C} \\ m & =1 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~kg}\end{aligned}$ $$ \begin{aligned} N & =\text { Number of turns }=5000 \\ I & =5 \mathrm{~A} \\ R & =\text { Radius of circular path of charge } \end{aligned} $$ $R=$ Radius of circular path of charge Magnetic field, $B=\mu_0 n l$ (where, $n=$ number of turns per unit length) $$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow B=\mu_0 \frac{N}{L} I (where, n=\frac{N}{L} )\\ & \Rightarrow B=\mu_0 \times \frac{5000}{L} \times 5 \\ & \Rightarrow B=\mu_0 \times \frac{25000}{L}...(i) \end{aligned} $$ Also we know that, Distance $=$ speed $\times$ time $L=v_x \times t$ (where, $v_x$ is the component of velocity along the axis of the solenoid)
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$ L=500 t...(ii) $$ Also, radius of circular path of a charged particle in uniform magnetic field is given by the formula $$ \begin{aligned} R & =\frac{m v_j}{9 B} \quad \text { (where, } v_y=500 \sqrt{3} \text { ) } \\ \text { Putting, } B & =\frac{\mu_0 \times 25000}{L} \quad \text { [from Eq .(i)] } \\ R & =\frac{10^{-11} \times 500 \sqrt{3} \times L}{10^{-16} \times \mu_0 \times 25000} \\ \Rightarrow \quad R & =\frac{10^{-11} \times \sqrt{3} L}{50 \mu_0} \quad \text {...(ii) } \end{aligned} $$ Now, time taken by the charged particle to exit the solenoid moving along the circular path making $N^{\prime}$ number of revolutions. $$ \begin{aligned} t^{\prime} & =\frac{\text { circular distance }}{v_y} \Rightarrow t^{\prime}=\frac{2 \pi R \times \text { no. of revolutions }}{v_y} \\ t^{\prime} & =\frac{2 \pi R \times N^{\prime}}{v_y} \\ t^{\prime} & =\frac{2 \pi \times 10^{-11} \times \sqrt{3} L \times N^{\prime}}{50 \mu_0 \times 500 \sqrt{3}} ...(iii)\end{aligned} $$ Now, since time taken to cover linear distance and circular distance before exiting the solenoid are same $$ t=t^{\prime} $$ So, from Eqs. (i) and (ii) $$ \frac{L}{500}=\frac{2 \pi \times 10^{-11} \times \sqrt{3} \times L \times N^{\prime}}{50 \times 4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \times 500 \sqrt{3}} \quad\left(\because \mu_0=4 \pi \times 10^{-7}\right) $$ On solving, we get $$ N^{\prime}=\frac{100 \times 10^{-7}}{10^{-11}}=\frac{10^{-5}}{10^{-11}}=10^6 $$ Hence, total number of revolution along helical path by the time it emerges the solenoid $=1 \times 10^6$. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A toroid has a core (non-ferro magnetic) of inner radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and outer radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>26</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> around which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2000</mn></math> turns of a wire is wound. If the current in the wire is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>, the magnetic field inside the core of the toroid is
questions.solution:
Given:

r1= 24 cmr2= 26 cm

Mean radius,

r=r1+r22=25 cm=14 m

Magnetic field inside the toroid,

B=μ0nI=μ0N2πrI=4π×10-720002π×14×12=1.92×10-2 T

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A long solenoid has 70 turns cm-1 and carries current I. An electron moves within the solenoid in a circle of radius 2.5 cm perpendicular to the solenoid axis. If the speed of the electron is 4.4×106 m s-1 then the current I in the solenoid is
(Take μ0=4π×10-7SI unit, mass of electron =9×10-31 kg, charge of electron =1.6×10-19C)
questions.solution:
Given:

n=70 turns cm-1=7000 turns m-1

and 

r=2.5 cm=0.025 m

Now centripetal force will be provided by the magnetic force acting on the charge. Therefore,

mv2r=qBv and we also know that for long solenoid,

B=μ0nI.

So,

mv2r=qμ0nIv⇒I=mv2μ0nr=9×10-314.4×10624π×10-770000.025=112.5 mA
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two similar equal poles magnet when separated by a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>, they repel with a force of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>. The pole strength is
questions.solution:
According to Question

Two similar equal poles magnet are d=1 m apart

Repel by force=F=10-3 N

Let both magnet have pole strength=m1=m2=m

μ0=4π×10-7 H m-1

Now Relation of forceF between two magnet and pole strengthm

F=μ04πm1m2d2

F=μ04πm2d2

m2=Fd2μ04π

m=Fd2μ04π

Put the all known values in the above relation

m=10-3×110-7⇒104

m=100 A m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
